terms.definition: Root Mean Squared Error — √(average squared residual) on held-out regression predictions; same units as target y.
Mean Absolute Error — average |y − ŷ| on held-out data; interpretable typical error in target units.
Mean Squared Error — average (y − ŷ)². Used as training loss in Loss Functions; RMSE = √MSE for evaluation.
Prediction error e = y − ŷ for one example; RMSE and MAE aggregate residuals across a scored dataset.
Spelled-out name for RMSE; common in engineering and forecasting literature.
Sum or mean of absolute residuals — MAE is the mean L1 error per example.
Sum or mean of squared residuals — MSE is the mean L2-squared error; RMSE is its square root.
